Root Cause (first observed failure):

action.hypothesis: `factorized_topk` has now failed for a concrete architecture-registration reason rather than an ambiguous quality or runtime signal: the execution layer rejects the architecture name in `SparseCoderConfig` before training begins. The next coherent move is a narrow code-fix round that wires `factorized_topk` into the existing config/class registry and forward path so the prototype can finally receive one real K=128 proxy measurement under the strongest current regime.
action.summary: Register and enable the `factorized_topk` architecture in `sparsify/`, then rerun the same K=128 proxy prototype under the current signum baseline.
action.self_review: This is the smallest defensible next step because the latest failure is an explicit compatibility bug, not a quality result, and continuing to retry without fixing registration would waste rounds. The main risk is that the family still underperforms once it runs, but one valid proxy result is required before deciding whether to stabilize or retire it.
notes_to_memory: Round 48 produced a concrete execution-layer error for `factorized_topk`: the architecture name is not registered in `SparseCoderConfig`, so the family still has no usable quality signal.
This is stronger evidence of a missing code integration step than of a bad architecture, so the family should not be retired on quality grounds yet.
This round keeps the original `factorized_topk` K=128 proxy target fixed and changes only the code needed to make the architecture selectable and runnable by the existing launcher.
next_hypotheses: If the registered `factorized_topk` run completes and beats plain `topk`, keep the family alive and begin stabilizing its internal factorization design.
If the run completes normally but remains far behind gated, retire shallow factorization and move to a different encoder-expressivity direction.
If `factorized_topk` still fails after registration is fixed, treat the remaining issue as a deeper implementation incompatibility and shift effort elsewhere.
